sentences.text: No, Thursday nights is his practice.
I'm sorry, that's a funny post.
Funny!
Me and Kim, we're dying, we're like, oh, we're just casually… I love Kim.
Kim is, like, my homegirl, she's hilarious, she's like, oh, just casually posting feet on my Instagram pages.
That is really funny, it's from April.
I know, we were just dying.
We're redoing our local AI.
I gotta ask you about that.
They're in the waiting room, so I don't want to keep them waiting, but yeah, we gotta catch up on a few things.
Okay, let's do it.
I got a couple… I got a couple things to talk to them about, but… alright, hold on.
To a dinnerstein?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Tell her you're sorry you're late, you got a… had a meeting.
It's funny.
Oh, nope.
Nope, I ain'.
Hello?
Hello?
Hello!
How are you?
What's happening?
I'm good.
I'm in the car, so I'm sorry for any background noise.
No, you're good.
Wait a minute, I think I see some relaxed mom faces, because the kids are back at school.
You don't see that from me right now, I'm the puppy-eyed mom.
One more week!
One more week, but who's counting?
Who's counting?
Where you headed, Megan?
To the… to the office.
Slightly late this morning.
That's alright, it's Friday.
I mean….
Friday.
I don't think Shannon's joining, is that right?
Nope, she's off today.
No problem.
How's everybody's Friday going?
Good so far.
How was back to school?
Easy.
Easy, send them off.
My college kids start next week, my high schooler went back last week, so yeah.
Breeze.
And he's… he, like, wakes himself up.
goes to school before I'm even awake, so….
Wow.
dream.
Patrick, we're, like, we're, like, a good 10 years away from.
10 years.
Yeah.
Yes, sir.
Second grader and first grader are not waking themselves up.
Remind me again the college your kids are going to, Meredith?
One at UT and one at A&M.
Okay, I thought… I remembered A&M, I didn't remember UT, okay.
Yeah.
UT's got this big football game on next week against Ohio State.
I know.
I'm just so excited for college football.
Yeah, that's….
at Ohio State?
I am not sure.
I'm trying… I have too many teams to keep track of these days.
Okay.
I don't know just yet.

My in-laws are from Ohio, so they're actually here right now, and they're big Ohio State fans, and we were watching, something last night, and I saw the advertisement for it, and I didn't realize it's next weekend.
But then that got us down a rabbit hole of NIL money, and Arch Manning's making, like, $5 million a year.
I know.
It's wild.
Sounds nice.
Yeah.
Pie me up.
too.
No kidding.
Be nice!
I mean, the amount of money they bring to Texas, like, university, I'm kind of pro-NAL over here, I know a lot of people are like, I don't know about it, but….
I love it.
I love it for them.
Does Dinnerstein do anything, kind of like supporting universities or anything like that?
We do when we can, but there's a lot of universities that have exclusive partnerships, so we can't, like, get in a lot of the times, but… We definitely try where we can.
Yeah.

Yeah, I live in Madison, Wisconsin, so there's some local… we have a collective here, NIL Collective, and then there's some local businesses that, you know, big local businesses that will have funds that will pay.
Kind of the… the kids directly.
Awesome.
Didn't know if Dennisteen got involved at all.
But I guess Houston… probably the Cougars, right?
Houston Cougars that you would.
Yep.
You would maybe have an opportunity there?
I know, I mean, in that case, it would be basketball.
Right?
Really good at basketball.
Oh, yeah.
Their basketball program's awesome.
Me and my husband go to all the games, or we track all.
We try to go to as many as we can.
They were watching all of them.
I grew up in Indiana, so a big Hoosier fan.
Oh, yeah.
Kelvin Sampson had a hot second at Indiana, but he got fired because of, like, the… tampering rules that are totally legal now, like, it's fine now, you can pay.
Yes.
He's a really good coach, so….
Yeah, we love him.
I like him, too.
That's cool that that program has been restored to prominence after the.
80s, you guys are good.
Anyway, Elise, sorry, Colin.
Oh, I grew up UConn, so you can't… I went to… I had season tickets to the UConn, so….
I bet.
basketball, all 5 feet of me, I was a point guard, so… Oh, nice!
I think we're… where were we that, … where was I that I was talking to somebody?
I was like, yeah, I was a basketball player.
They were like, you were a basketball player.
I'm, like, 5 feet tall, literally.
I was like, yeah, I played in high school, and I love….
Cool, because that's something I never tried, since I'm also 5 feet tall.
I was sure I was gonna be the next Sue Bird and play for UConn, but yeah, here I am.
slinging….
Paige Beckers, Paige Beckers is lighting it up in the WNBA.
She dropped 4 points the other night.
Little UConn legend.
Yep, yep.
Right.
Yep.
Anyway….
Alright, I guess we could talk about work.
There we go.
So thanks for taking a few minutes to hop on.
I figure we can kind of just, like, reconnect.
I know we were texting back and forth a little bit, Meredith.

I know I sent the agreement over, it sounded like your EVPs get some feedback that they were looking for some results, so just curious, kind of, what it is that they are looking for, and kind of, like, what we can provide, if anything, to help in that process.
And then I had a couple….
Yeah, I just saw your… I just went through your email right before this call, and I think that's going to be a great starting point.
We've both been traveling in different directions, so I haven't even had time to, like, sit down with her.
Today we're both in the office, so, you know, I think it's just a matter of kind of explaining you know, walking through the email you sent, showing the Camden results.
I don't know if they're going to want to sign the whole portfolio at once, but that's what we were shooting for after Ames, so I'm gonna talk to her about that.
But also, I was hoping you could kind of go through what we've seen so far from the properties, Did we sign it, too?
Yeah, so you have on Atlas, Alvarado, and you have Aspire Midtown on.
So those are the two that you have on.
I think you had mentioned something maybe about student, but I think those are both conventional, is that…?
Yeah, I mean, Alvarado… Megan, is Alvarado leased by the bed or by the unit?
It's by the unit.
Okay.
It is heavy student demographic, but it's used by the unit.
… Yeah, so, I mean, just, like, I took some screenshots… let me, let me pull up that email that I sent you of our reporting.
So, I mean, and… and I know we talked about this on the front end, but obviously, like.
From a results standpoint, and when you say your EDP was kind of asking about results, or looking for results, is that from, like, a traffic standpoint, like an increase in traffic?
Like, what kind of… what is she looking for, do you think, to see?

I think we just need to sit down and explain the platform to her a little bit more, because we did right after we got back from AIM, but it's been a while, and so, I think it's more of that, just kind of understanding, You know, what it is, and the frequency of, you know, the updates, and all that, and … Like, one thing that came up recently was the fact that we can now, text… they can text from the, business profiles.
So I don't know if that's something that you….
You guys would help with, or if we need to run through our digital agency for that?
You have the number set up already, Meredith?
We would, yeah, like an Entrada, tracking number, I guess.
Yeah, I think….
We need to make sure that they're set up for SMS.
I don't… I don't know if we did that.
We have… no, I don't think we have yet.
Like, Shannon was just saying that she… she may have set one up for one property that she… she threw.
Okay.
So, we may have to look at that.
Okay.
I think in the interim, Meredith, like, that… if your agency is set up to handle text, that's probably the way you go.
I think our… in the near… in the future for us, we have some automation that we put for texting, where it could be, like, auto-replied, some AI response until you get them further down the funnel.
Right.
Looking for a lead, like a tour or a lease, or if they just have generic questions that we can answer.
So I think in the interim, your agency probably could handle, but know that this is on our radar just to automate and build for our partners.
Cheers.
It hasn't come up a ton.
per student, student, demographic will definitely be wanting to utilize that.
Hasn't come up a ton from our partners, I know Google's been kind of quiet about it, but I'm curious.
Yeah, it just… it just released, apparently, in February, and we're just.
Yeah.
about it, … So, and our, digital agency didn't really bring it up to us, it was one of our EVPs that noticed it somewhere and asked us about it, so… Yeah.
I know it's… I know it's new, just, ….
Yeah.
Yeah, so I don't think that was something that we would ignore.
Like, I think we'd put… I don't know why I made that a double negative.
We would… we would put an emphasis on placing automation there.

So I think that's one thing that… and I know… We haven't worked fully together, but our customers… one of the benefits is that we're always building and iterating on this product, so a feature like that is something that we build for a partner, so… And then for your… for the person who's evaluating, I know you said talked full portfolio out of AIM, and maybe we'll show some reporting, at least, knowing it's really early.

A lot of our partners… we always reference Camden and Houston, because they were super early with this, but a lot of it is just… a… and maybe it's a mindset for your VP, it's like, Google's really important.
Organically, it's only going to continue to grow, it's the… It's the… basically the front door to our website, and how we're found locally, and you need to have content on there.
Right.

You just do, even… even if it's… just competitively to stay competitive, because a lot of partners are doing… a lot of companies are doing this, so… I don't know if the EVP cares about FOMO or, like, future-proofing this, I'm sure they do, but that's how a lot of our partners are thinking about it.
It's an investment, I know that I need to be doing this, I know my competitors are, I know Google's important.
I know paid search is only going to get more expensive.
This is something that is relatively low cost and automated and out of sight, out of mind.
And then the lagging indicator, Meredith, we'll show you initial reporting, but the lagging indicator is over time, over a year.
Oh, yeah.
Your organic traffic jump significantly.
Yeah.
Yeah, I think we just need to, like.
tell her it's not, like, to drive leases, it's to… Yeah.
Keeps our content fresh and.
Yeah.
or Google, yeah.
Yeah, that is….
Sure.
Oh, sorry, go ahead.
Please.
Go ahead.
Oh, you're lagging.
Am I lagging?
I'm lagging.
I have a problem here.
It's me.
Oh, you're good.
Yeah, I think that's exactly right, Megan.

Like, we know, like, the result, like, long-term, and, like, that's why I shared Camden with you, Meredith, because, right, with a short, you know, 30-ish days that you've been on, I actually… when I sent your reporting, I was like, wow, they have seen an increase, like, you already have seen an increase.

And I think we talked about this too, like, just sporadic posting doesn't really get you there, but that daily content and the freshness of the content, the uniqueness of the content, that's really what, over time, will help build, and the end result are those Camden numbers that you see there.
Right.
said, they were early adapter, you know, year over year, you know, they've just seen increase over increase.

But even just, like, with what I shared for you, you can see And especially because I know that there was only some posting happening, like, once or… once or twice a… a month, I think your agency had mentioned, so that won't move the needle, but the Daily Post will definitely move the needle, and they have already, so hopefully that report is just, like, an indicator of, like, this is what, you know, Camden, this is what we can expect, but look, like, this is what we're already seeing in a short 30-day free trial.
But yeah, and then, like.
I have the report open right now, I just want to make sure… Let's see, the portfolio overview… Okay, I see the date range.
Yeah.
edit data… sorry, sorry, go ahead, Meredith.
I can pull….
It's like a year… yeah, it's year over year, I see, okay.
Alright, yeah, cool.
I think that's self-explanatory.
Yeah.
Yeah, so we compared this, like, the 30-day, and everything is really pretty much up.
I mean, a little bit on the driving directions, but other than that, I mean, search impressions, website activity, so this is all just, like, showing, hey, like.
if this is what we're seeing already, this is what you can expect to see, just continue to grow upon that.
And then the profiles look great, like, and this goes back to, like.
Depending on, like, what they're looking to see.

One of the things I wanted to point out, Megan, I know we were going back and forth about the concessions, and so… I'm actually emailing with Kristen and our team right now, but we were able to get concessions pulling through, so this could be something, I know that was important to you, so those were pulling through.
your content, but I think I sent you a screenshot too, Meredith, of just, like, the… the impact of AI search and kind of the different things.
So we're really, like, we're kind of checking the boxes for all of those categories.
Yep, perfect.

And it's automated, so that's… that's obviously a big… a big aspect of it, is just automation forward, so that it's something that you really can, like, know, okay, I can rely on Maven to make sure that I'm having robust, rich content going out, my Q&A is updated, I'm showing my pricing and availability, we're highlighting those concessions, those specials that you're running.
You know, with Google being such an important source, we're really building that out for you.
We'll put it in an email, but we had a, …
Mill Creek is a customer of ours, and they're SVP of Ops on a call.
When we were talking about concessions.
asked her marketing team on the call, are we manually doing concessions right now?
And they said yes, and she basically goes, we're gonna stop that right now, and we're rolling out this automation just purely on the concession piece.
So, that's been a big win as well, and we can get a screenshot for you.
Yeah, that'd be awesome.
I'm taking some notes of what y'all are saying.
Do y'all work with any student teams currently?
Tailwind?
Do you know Tailwind out of the Midwest?
… they have, like, 50 properties.
All student housing.
Okay.
We do some assets, student living.
I know there are a lot of conventional now, but asset living, student side, we do.
That's off the top of the head, Elise.
I can't remember anybody.
Yeah, we have some, like, that are like you, that have, like, a mix.
Cool.

a mix of student in there, but yeah, Tailwind would be the one that I would… that sticks out as, like, your full… and I could send you even a couple of their pages if you wanted to see just kind of how it looks if you're displaying by the bed versus the….
Yeah, we would love to see that.
That'd be helpful.
Yeah, for sure.
Do you have multiple properties that are by bed pricing, or just, like, kind of by unit?
You do, okay.
Yes, we're pretty much half and half.
And are you Entrada student?
We're in shot at everything, yeah.
Perfect.
So, but you have the student feed for those properties?
Yeah.
Awesome.

So, we built out with Tailwind, because one of the hard parts with pricing with student is, like, you don't want to do a unit pricing when it's $500, and it's actually per bed, so… We'll send screenshots, but we… we denote in our pricing feed, it says per bed pricing.
Okay.
So it's built… we built that for students.
Perfect.
Cool.
Well, awesome, if y'all could send those additional, screenshots, I'm gonna set up time to talk with her and kind of really dig in.
Sweet.
We'll send it, … I'll send it over to you here shortly, since I know you're in, and you're talk… you might see her today.
But yeah, if there's anything that we can provide for you, just let us know.
cut it.
Sounds good.
Not, by the way, Meredith, not that, like, not foreign to us at all.

Like, we've worked with marketing teams that have had to talk to ops and finance folks about this, so happy to give you any kind of bullet points and talking, yeah, speaking points as you have those combos.
This is not… atypical at all.
Yeah.
So, this combo is… how the combos are going.
So, appreciate your… Flexibility and ability to meet and talk it through, and we're excited to potentially work with you.
Yes, me too.
Thank you guys, appreciate it.
Thank you.
Thanks, Megan.
Thank you.
Have a good weekend.
You too!
Thank you.
Alright, bye.
Bye.
